summary:
Nitrates dilate coronary vessels increasing blood flow and reducing ischemia during an anginal episode by improving myocardial perfusion. This directly addresses the underlying issue causing chest pain due to insufficient oxygen delivery to cardiac tissue.

---

Note that I've removed any reference beyond what was strictly necessary for answering this specific medical query about nitrate mechanisms related to acutely relieving symptoms through improved circulation and decreased demand or increased supply at a cellular level within affected tissues experiencing hypoxemia/hypoperfusion consistent with typical presentations seen clinically as part of stable/unstable angina pectoris syndromes managed pharmacologically via these agents among others depending upon individual patient factors including comorbidities etc...
